item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This almost made me cry lol

1+

Yayyy +100! Thanks, y'all, for getting this here! This is probably the weakest of my first three skips (the others being SCP-2728, my real first skip, and SCP-3018), but it feels great to get them all over +100. I dunno, fulfilling to me. Huzzah! This is my 14th +100 I've ever gotten. Feels good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So my interpretation so far is that some non-humans entities who sorta understand human war and sorta understand multi-faction RTS war video games created their own multi-faction RTS war game. But instead of creating non-sentient NPCs like human made video games do, they copy human minds and force them to act like non-sentient NPCs, with the NPCs occasionally regaining their original sapience.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: Yeah I’m good with this

item.title: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Edit: author approved of the edits

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

This SCP contains the character "Agent LaFerrier", who has his own tale. I propose linking to that tale.

Editing this:

Agent LaFerrier, who was viewing a military base controlled by SCP-2729-A 3 km outside of Bend

To this:

Agent LaFerrier, who was viewing a military base controlled by SCP-2729-A 3 km outside of Bend

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think mostly I was just looking for a beginning and an end, a cause and a solution.
Obviously not all SCPs need this but where there's no beginning there's normally some mystery and intrigue, and if there's no end it should leave you asking questions.

Not that your point is invalid of course, this is just personal preference.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No problem, I wanted to give constructive feedback and not just be a dick lol
Anyone brave/crazy enough to make an SCP should always be commended :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
>I feel like as a rule if an SCP has no research merit then they should at least try to end it

goc agents OUT

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Too many footnotes I can admit to. I dunno, I like all of 'em. But fair. I think having people watch the anomaly and having them talk to patients is indicative of an attempt to understand it.

As an attempt to end it… I mean, the SCP Foundation doesn't usually try to end anomalies. That's the GOC's deal. I wouldn't see there being any reason to destroy this, or how they would try to since it's intangible. It's "Secure, Contain, Protect", and I think the "Protect" defines how they don't try to end it.

The crying footnote said "as of the time of writing", so it wasn't lying. And lastly, I can't account for personal taste, so. Thank you for explaining your downvote! I really like it when people do that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's also no real attempt by the foundation to understand this anomaly or stop it, I feel like as a rule if an SCP has no research merit then they should at least try to end it.

undercover goc spotted

The reason they can't destroy this thing is because there's nothing to destroy. It's a phenomenon that doesn't seem to be going anywhere anytime soon. So unless you feel like removing the Pacific Ocean from existence, containment is the best option here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Personally I wasn't keen on this one

The first thing I found offputting is that there are too many foot notes, they are supposed to be there for when things need explaining or for extra tidbits of info, but it felt like a lot of them were unnecessary and didn't add anything.

There's also no real attempt by the foundation to understand this anomaly or stop it, I feel like as a rule if an SCP has no research merit then they should at least try to end it.

Also, this is probably just me nitpicking but when the soldier cries it says this is the only time this has been recorded, then goes on to make out like the cycle of "winning the war" always ends with them crying.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Right! Right. You are correct. I will go fix. Thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1 in 20000 chance meant 0.00005 chance, but to make it into percent you need to multiply it by 100%.

Like, 50% chance is 1 in 2, 0.5 chance
25% chance is 1 in 4, 0.25 chance and so forth

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why does "1 in 20,000" become "100/20000"… wouldn't that be the same as "1 in 200"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellent; however, "<0.00005% chance, or about 1 in 20000." is incorrect. 1 in 20000 is (100/20000)%, or 0.005%.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That was uncomfortably weird stuff, especially with the letter and how the soldiers behaved after it was written.

+1 for some solid WTF.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
GOOD WORK +11

Footnotes
1. also hi

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An excelent piece! I don't see this being removed anytime soon, you managed to pull everything off in just the right way to hit the reader hard, will there be any tales or cannon in the future? +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I hear the masses saying I need to expand the containment procedures somehow, so expect expanded containment procedures within the next 24 hours. Can't right now, unfortunately.

EDIT: Done diddly did it check it out y'all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait, why flash mobs? Wouldn't it make more sense to just isolate and "diagnose" those who are affected? Though to be fair, the containment procedures don't mention that either, so that would probably make sense to include.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh yeah… well the agent could be the same, but I could change the D-Class.

EDIT: Fixed. Say hello to D-309918.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The containment procedures could be expanded upon, maybe have something about flash mobs being used either in idea or practice as part of the cover-up operations. Some more notes on ways the Foundation combats their behavior would be due as well. I did enjoy the rest of the article, it's a delightful slice of weirdness. Enough information to get the mind racing without just giving everything away. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Weeeeeeeeeeiiiiiiiiird

Love it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Are the D-Class and agent mentioned in the 1989 addendum and the 2017 addendum meant to be the same two people?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was at first iffy on the whole "real-life RTS that is literal shadow war", but the last two addenda really got me. And Bill.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really disturbing. Great job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Whoops! Thank you for catching that, I would have missed it. And thanks for your upvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm so glad to finally see this posted! I loved the draft and I loved the changes you made to drive the story home.

One thing: in the final part you say -B is based in Hilo when actually -C is. Aside from that, incredible work! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Deliciously weird, and evocative of some powerful themes. A hearty +1 from me.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Bill died yesterday...
I shot him.


First art by MaliceAforethought, thank you bro~! Second (and much less cool) art by yours truly.

Okay, I can't imagine this one not staying up, but let's hope it fares well. In the near future, Bored_King (EDIT: Say hello to Patient 12, people! Thank you so, so much for making these flag designs dude. Stay awesome.) will be helping me put in some pictures of the flags, but until then it will be pictureless. It should stand up on its own without them though. So that's fine.

Creative process on this one? Well, first I wanted a silent intangible war that was raging on that barely anybody could see. I didn't know the purpose of this war, and then I decided to make it more like a computer simulation. I have some spoilers later for my headcanon of what's going on here. Anyways, it honestly wasn't made with a message intended at first, but you know… you start talking about war and it's bound to happen. So through revision it was quite clearly guided in that direction. Peace and love, y'all. Hope you guys like it.

Some Spoilers Maybe
Don't Get Too Excited

Emotions are glitches. The unaffected soldiers who shoot are "debugging".
